Think of it this way, it's like a virtual factory.  The law considers knowledge, lines of code, all of that to be imported into Paraguay as if it were raw material.  There, it is transformed into the final product, which is the software, and then this product is exported to customers worldwide.  And this structure was created precisely to cover the main activities of the digital economy.  So, we're talking about app software development, data processing and analysis, BPO and KPO services, and even research and development.  The calculation is quite straightforward. The 1% tax will be levied on whichever value is greater: either the total local costs, such as wages and services in Paraguay, or the total value of the export invoice. What makes the Maquila law so powerful is the comprehensive package of benefits that comes with it, because the program was designed to eliminate several other very common taxes.
